item.title: UGC to manage all grants now on, govt unifies research funding
item.description: The government has decided to merge the two research funding allocations – one for the University Grants Commission and the other for the public universities – with all grants henceforth to be channelled directly through the commission in a move aimed at preventing alleged......Maps

item.title: Argentina await FIFA decision over displaying Falklands banner
item.description: Football’s global governing body FIFA will assess match reports before deciding if they take any action over Argentina’s players holding up a banner reading ‘Las Malvinas son Argentinas’ (The Falklands are Argentine) following their 2-1 victory over England....

item.description: Unidentified individuals illegally boarded a vessel in the Gulf of Aden, a maritime security agency reported on Friday, with Yemen’s coast guard.....

item.description: China warned on Friday it could take ‘countermeasures’ after Washington finalised rules shortening how longjournalistscan stay in the United States, with Chinese nationals facing the strictest limits....

item.description: Nigeria said it has completed the evacuation of its citizens from South Africa this week, airlifting nearly 1,500 people as anti-immigrant groups.....

item.description: Days of relentless rainfall have battered nearly 180 kilometres of roads in Bagerhat, causing an estimated Tk 80 crore in damage to roads and transport infrastructure and disrupting traffic....

item.description: Gazipur Agricultural University has launched its first-ever three-month basic Japanese and Japanese language proficiency test preparation programme, strengthening students’ global communication skills, international employability and academic competitiveness....

item.description: A tornado struck the Sugandha Point of Cox’s Bazar beach on Friday afternoon, but no casualties occurred in the storm lasting for several minutes....

item.title: Youths urge action to protect children from lead exposure
item.description: Environmental activists, students, teachers and community members on Friday called for urgent action against lead pollution in Rangpur, warning that unsafe lead-acid battery recycling and other sources of exposure are putting millions of children at serious health risk....

item.description: Vice-chancellor of the National University Professor ASM Amanullah has announced comprehensive structural reforms to restore academic excellence, criticising the systemic politicisation that hampered the country’s education system over the past 17 years....

item.description: The ongoing Higher Secondary Certificate and equivalent examinations in all districts under the Chattogram division would remain postponed until further notice, according to the education ministry....

item.description: Agriculture, fisheries and livestock minister Mohammad Aminur Rashid on Friday announced that the government will provide all necessary support, including free paddy seeds and seedlings, to help flood-affected farmers in five districts of the Chattogram division resume agricultural activities....

item.description: At Goyla-Ekdala Punarbashan Para, an informal settlement on government land in Sirajganj municipality that is home to around 600 families displaced by river erosion, access to safe water remains a major challenge....

item.title: Power price not hiked for low-income consumers: energy minister
item.description: Power, energy and mineral resources minister Iqbal Hassan Mahmood Tuku on Friday said that electricity price had not been increased for consumers using up to 75 units a month to protect low-income households from additional financial burdens....

item.description: The International Organisation for Migration has stepped up emergency relief operations in Bangladesh as weeks of intense rainfall, flooding and landslides have triggered one of the country’s worst monsoon disasters this year, affecting more than......ExecutiveBranch

item.description: The National Board of Revenue has dismissed an additional commissioner of customs from service after she remained absent from duty for more than 60 days without approval and allegedly left for Australia without obtaining the required leave....

item.description: The European Union demanded Thursday that Google share data with other search engines and open up its Android system to rival AI services, but the US giant said the measures would put privacy at risk....

item.description: Huddled together shoulder-to-shoulder, Gen-Z hires at a Tokyo firm roared ‘Hell yeah!’ to cap their morning rally, displaying the kind of corporate machismo that fuelled Japan’s post-war economic miracle....

item.description: Alexander Quesada was gifted a tractor for turning a Cuban rubbish dump into a model farm, but these days he is using oxen to pull his plough as a severe energy crisis worsened by a US blockade hobbles food production....

item.description: Netflix shares dropped as much as 8 per cent Thursday after results underwhelmed investors already worried about slowing engagement at the streaming giant....

item.title: Breaking hundi cycle
item.description: INFORMAL remittance networks have been part of South Asia’s economic landscape for generations. Known variously as hundi, hawala or chiti, these systems existed long before modern banking became widespread. Today, despite advances in financial technology and......

item.title: Building safer healthcare system
item.description: AFTER every major healthcare tragedy in Bangladesh, the pattern of public response follows: shock, grief, investigations, suspensions, legal proceedings and promises of reform. When the public attention moves away, the systemic weaknesses often remain unaddressed. Investigations and......

item.title: Tackling digital colonialism
item.description: COLONIALISM has always been about control over resources. In the twenty-first century, however, the most valuable resource is no longer land or raw materials but data. The rise of global technology giants has given birth to what is increasingly described as digital colonialism — a system in which.......

item.title: Social cost of climate inaction
item.description: SINCE early July 2026, relentless monsoon rain has inundated seven districts in the southeast — Chattogram, Cox’s Bazar, Bandarban, Rangamati, Khagrachhari, Moulvibazar and Habiganj. More than a million people have been affected, nearly 268,000 households stranded, almost......

item.title: Recurring crop losses call for lasting solutions
item.description: WIDESPREAD damage to standing crops caused by recent floods is alarming. Heavy rainfall and flash floods in July 6–14 damaged crops on 27,011 hectares of farmland in seven districts, affecting the livelihood of more than 211,000 farmers, as a preliminary official assessment......

item.description: THE July uprising, which ended the authoritarian rule of the Awami League, was driven by aspirations for far-reaching state reforms, including curbing executive powers, safeguarding judicial independence and human rights and ensuring the accountability of constitutional and......

item.description: Representatives of civil society, academia, cultural organisations and political groups at a rally on Friday described Bangladesh’s recent trade agreement with the United States as exploitative, alleging that it would undermine people’s rights and threaten businesses, farmers and workers....News

item.description: Dhaka ranked 27th among the world’s most polluted cities on Friday morning, recording an Air Quality Index (AQI) score of 84 at 11:06am. ...News
